overview_summary: Of the four neighboring landowners profiled, none have a documented public stance on development or clean energy (all are classified as "unknown" stance). All appear to have a low public profile with no notable official positions or community leadership roles, meaning none are considered high-influence figures in local affairs.

neighbors.name: Josue & Diana Flores
neighbors.claims: County assessor records list Josue and Diana Flores as the owners of parcel 106-06-019A in Cochise County [Cochise County Assessor](https://www.cochise.az.gov/178/Assessor). No references to the Flores family were found in local news or public meeting archives, suggesting they have not taken any prominent public role or stance on area developments [Cochise County Voice](https://cochisecountyvoice.com).

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage the Floreses with a personal, neighborly conversation emphasizing transparency about the project. Listening to any concerns in an informal setting can build trust given their low public profile.

neighbors.name: Loren J. Sheldon
neighbors.claims: Loren J. Sheldon is listed as the owner of parcel 106-06-020 in county property records, indicating a privately held tract in the area [Cochise County Assessor](https://www.cochise.az.gov/178/Assessor). There is no public record of Sheldon serving in local government or voicing opinions in local media about development, implying a very low public profile in community affairs [Cochise County Voice](https://cochisecountyvoice.com).

approach_recommendations.engage: Outreach should be one-on-one and respectful of Sheldon’s privacy. Emphasize that local input is valued and provide clear information to address any questions, as he has not publicly engaged on such issues.

neighbors.name: Richard E. & Julia Ann Wilson
neighbors.claims: Richard E. Wilson and Julia Ann Wilson jointly own parcel 106-06-018P in Cochise County, according to county assessment records [Cochise County Assessor](https://www.cochise.az.gov/178/Assessor). Neither Richard nor Julia Ann appears in local news reports or public meeting minutes, indicating they have not publicly weighed in on development matters and hold no notable public positions [Cochise County Voice](https://cochisecountyvoice.com).

approach_recommendations.engage: A courteous, face-to-face approach through a community liaison or neighbor is advised. The Wilsons may appreciate a focus on how the project will protect local property values and maintain the quiet rural character they likely enjoy.

neighbors.name: Donald Franz
neighbors.claims: Donald Franz is identified in county records as the owner of parcel 106-06-029A in the area [Cochise County Assessor](https://www.cochise.az.gov/178/Assessor). No evidence was found of Franz participating in local civic activities or voicing opinions on land development in media or meetings, pointing to a private individual with no public stance on such projects [Cochise County Voice](https://cochisecountyvoice.com).

approach_recommendations.engage: Engage Mr. Franz through a neighborly visit or phone call that stresses respect for his property and any personal priorities. Since he hasn’t been publicly involved, a low-key, informative approach can help address any concerns privately.
